whatThisIs: Score the four-question CAGE alcohol-use disorder screen: have you ever felt the need to Cut down on drinking, been Annoyed by criticism of your drinking, felt Guilty about drinking, or needed a morning drink (Eye-opener) to steady nerves or treat a hangover. Each yes is one point. A score of 2 or more is the standard positive cutoff and warrants a more detailed alcohol-use assessment such as AUDIT or the SBIRT brief intervention pathway.
whenToUse: Use this during a primary-care or behavioral-health intake, during a pre-procedure or pre-operative evaluation when withdrawal risk needs to be triaged, or when an ED patient presents with a syndrome (trauma, GI bleed, pancreatitis, unexplained AST/ALT ratio above 2) that should prompt alcohol-use screening. The CAGE is brief and well-validated for detecting alcohol-use disorder, but it is less sensitive for at-risk drinking patterns; pair it with AUDIT-C or AUDIT-10 when the goal is to catch heavy drinking that has not yet caused functional impairment.
